qa75_fixes_verification.note: QA #75 source data unavailable for direct diff. Evidence in current code suggests handleWorkerClick/handleTagClick/onResume/syncFromGitHub are wrapped in useCallback (fixed), aria-checked added to role='radio' buttons (partial fix), ErrorBoundary present, confetti timer race fixed. handleCharClick and handleTabKeyDown remain unwrapped — these appear to have been missed in the QA #75 pass.

suggestions.title: NEW: Bloberto speech bubble hard-clipped at top of office floor
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1568 places Bloberto at top={4} (4% from top of .office-floor). The speech bubble uses position:absolute; bottom:calc(100%+8px) (Office.css line 749). office-floor has overflow:hidden (Office.css line 84). At 4% top with a 4/3 aspect-ratio container at max-width 900px, the floor is ~675px tall — 4% = ~27px. Bubble height at 0.65rem font with 160px max-width can be 35-50px, so it always overflows. CONFIRMED in screenshot-sofia-desktop-77.png: 'we're exactly on track.' is visibly clipped. Fix: add overflow:visible to the manager character wrapper, or increase Bloberto's top position to at least 10-12%, or apply a CSS clip-path exception for the manager Character's z-layer.

suggestions.title: handleCharClick not wrapped in useCallback — defeats memo() on ALL Character components
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1249: const handleCharClick = (worker) => { ... } is a plain function defined inside the Office component body. It is passed as onClick to every Character component (lines 1568, 1583, 1613). Character is wrapped in React.memo(), but memo() bails on every render of Office because handleCharClick is a new reference each time. Fix: wrap in useCallback(() => {...}, [onWorkerClick]) — no dependencies are missing since pingTimerRef and pingReactionTimerRef are refs. This is the single highest-leverage perf fix available. Strike 4 counting description history from prior reviews.

suggestions.title: Footer quote counter vanishes at last quote AND wraps silently
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1448: setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1). BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 items. Counter display condition: quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length hides the counter at quoteBonus=8 (8 < 8 is false). User clicks at 7/8, sees 7/8, clicks again — counter disappears instead of showing 8/8 then cycling. The wrap resets to 1 silently with no visual feedback that quotes are cycling. Fix: change display condition to quoteBonus > 0 and show a ↺ icon when wrapping; or change the inequality to quoteBonus <= BONUS_TAGLINES.length.

suggestions.title: NEW: StatsBar (live dot, error count, stuck workers, sync time) buried in Dashboard tab only
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1296: StatsBar is rendered exclusively inside the tabpanel-dashboard div. The default tab is 'office'. On the Office tab — the primary view — users see ZERO data freshness indicators: no live/offline dot, no last-synced timestamp, no stuck worker alert, no error count. The header has the vibe pill and a sync button but no live-data status. Users have no idea if the office view is stale or if there are errors unless they manually switch to Dashboard. Fix: move a minimal status strip (live dot + last synced + error badge) to either the header or just above the Office tabpanel, keeping the full StatsBar in Dashboard.

suggestions.title: localStorage.getItem calls in useState initializers are unguarded
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 702: useState(() => localStorage.getItem('bloberto-theme') || 'dark') and line 749: useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen')). Both call localStorage directly with no try/catch. safeSave() wraps writes correctly (utils/safeSave.js) but reads are unguarded. localStorage.getItem throws SecurityError in iOS private browsing, Firefox with strict privacy, and some enterprise browser policies. If thrown during React's lazy initializer, the component never mounts and shows a blank screen. Fix: wrap reads in try/catch or use a safeRead utility analogous to safeSave.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard workerHistory allocates a new reversed array on every render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 312 (inside WorkerCard): const workerHistory = [...activityEntries].reverse() creates a new array on every render. WorkerCard is React.memo'd, but activityEntries is a slice from activityByWorker (a memoized Map), so the array reference is stable between unrelated renders — EXCEPT that reverse() mutates the copy in-place and returns it, meaning the spread is necessary. Still, this runs even for non-activity-related re-renders. Fix: useMemo(() => [...activityEntries].reverse(), [activityEntries]) inside WorkerCard. Or compute it in activityByWorker's useMemo and store the reversed version directly.

suggestions.title: handleTabKeyDown missing useCallback — new reference on every App render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 788: const handleTabKeyDown = (e) => { ... } is defined in the App component body without useCallback. It is passed as onKeyDown to both tab buttons (lines 1272, 1285). The function closes over tab and TABS — both stable enough to make useCallback(fn, [tab]) correct. Without it, every App state change (polling tick, worker update, etc.) hands a new function reference to the tab buttons, defeating any memoization on their containing elements. Fix: wrap in useCallback with [tab] in the dependency array.

suggestions.title: button[role='radio'] semantic conflict in activity filter bars — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1393-1402 and 1404-1423: <button role='radio' aria-checked={...}>. ARIA spec allows role=radio on non-input elements only when inside a radiogroup and only when keyboard interaction matches the radio pattern (arrow keys navigate, Space selects). These buttons only handle onClick — no onKeyDown for arrow-key navigation. Screen readers announce them as radio buttons, but arrow keys do nothing; Tab moves through them individually. Either: (a) implement onKeyDown arrow-key navigation within the radiogroup, or (b) switch to role='button' with aria-pressed — semantically simpler and already correctly implemented for toggle buttons.

suggestions.title: Dashboard activity log double-filters activityLog inline on every render
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1369-1375 and 1426-1435: both the count display and ActivityLog component call activityLog.filter(...).filter(...) as inline expressions in JSX. These run on every render of the App component (polling ticks every 30s, cursor moves, etc.). With activityLog capped at 50 entries this is cheap, but it allocates two new arrays per filter pass per render unnecessarily. Fix: derive filteredActivity = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) and use it in both places.

suggestions.title: NEW: Conference table 'last huddle' text rendered at 0.38rem — physically unreadable
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1002-1006: <div style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.38rem', ... }}> renders the last huddle recap (names + relative time). At browser default 16px root, 0.38rem = 6.08px. This is below the WCAG minimum of 9px for decorative text and well below any reasonable readability threshold — it is effectively invisible. This appears to be an accidental value rather than intentional (compare to nearby 0.4rem values which are also tiny but at least legible with squinting). Fix: raise to 0.6rem minimum, or move the last-huddle info into a tooltip on the conference table.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable setTick re-render anti-pattern — entire component re-renders every 30s
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 910: const [, setTick] = useState(0) with setInterval every 30s when meetingWorkers.length >= 2. The entire ConferenceTable re-renders including all SVG chair elements, the huddle timer text, and the CharacterAvatar components for each meeting participant. The only time-dependent value is the huddle duration display (line 1013: Math.floor((Date.now() - startedAt) / 60_000)). Fix: move the huddle duration into its own tiny component with its own tick state, or use useTimeTick(30_000) from App.jsx which is already designed for this pattern. ConferenceTable itself would then be stable.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble emoji selected via Date.now() at render time — flickers on re-renders
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 813: the idle bubble emoji is selected via Math.floor((Date.now() / 12000) + hash) % length at render time inside JSX. Because this expression runs each render, any unrelated state change during the 2.2s display window will re-evaluate the formula with a different Date.now() value, potentially swapping the emoji mid-animation. Fix: capture the chosen emoji in the setIdleBubble call (store the string instead of a boolean) so the displayed emoji is stable for the full 2.2s duration.

suggestions.title: Bloberto ping reactions are generic corporate platitudes — no personality
suggestions.description: PING_REACTIONS['bloberto'] at Office.jsx line 217: ['syncing team velocity', 'the roadmap says we are on track', 'ship it and iterate', 'every sprint tells a story', 'have you checked the velocity chart']. Every other character has sharp, funny, character-specific reactions. Bloberto's reads like a parody of bad management speak that isn't quite self-aware enough to land. Given the overall quality of the copy elsewhere (LONE_SURVIVOR_WORKER_OVERRIDES, SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES, CLOCK_COMMENTS), these are noticeably weak. Suggested replacements with actual personality: 'the metrics are vibing', 'i believe in your velocity', 'roadmap says yes', 'we're exactly on track (i made the chart)', 'synergy achieved'.

suggestions.title: NEW: isTouchDevice evaluated at module load time — won't reflect input changes
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 687: const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches is a module-level constant evaluated once when the module is first imported. It doesn't respond to MediaQueryList change events. While input method switching is rare in practice, this pattern can cause stale state on hybrid touch/mouse devices and prevents SSR/test environments from controlling it. Fix: move inside the App component body (or a useMemo), and optionally subscribe to the MediaQueryList change event for live updates.

suggestions.title: idleWorkers filter has redundant !workingIds.has(w.id) guard
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1222: nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'idle' && !workingIds.has(w.id)). workingIds is derived from workers with status === 'working'. A worker cannot simultaneously have status 'idle' AND be in workingIds (which only contains status 'working' workers) — the status field is mutually exclusive. The !workingIds.has check always evaluates true for idle workers. The guard implies distrust in the data model. Fix: remove the redundant guard. If there is genuine concern about malformed data, add a data validation step at the normalization layer in syncFromGitHub instead.

suggestions.title: ActivityLogEntry key uses string concatenation — fragile and non-unique
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 151: key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i}. Three issues: (1) string concatenation is fragile — timestamp '2026-03-10T23:00:00Z' + worker 'Carlos' could theoretically collide with timestamp '2026-03-10T23:00:00ZCarlos' + empty worker. (2) The index i is appended as fallback, which re-introduces index-as-key fragility. (3) Timestamp is an ISO string — mixing it with strings without a separator invites subtle bugs. Fix: key={`${entry.timestamp}::${entry.worker ?? ''}::${entry.type}::${i}`} with explicit separators, or better yet ensure activity entries have stable unique IDs in the data source.

suggestions.title: NEW: lone-survivor-label uses all-inline styles — maintenance smell
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1588: the lone-survivor-label <div> has 9 inline style properties (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color). A className 'lone-survivor-label' is already present but contains none of these styles — they're entirely inline. This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses CSS classes for layout/visual properties. Fix: move all 9 properties into a .lone-survivor-label rule in Office.css. Minor but part of a pattern of progressive inline-style creep seen in plantToast (Office.jsx:1334) and ConferenceTable lastHuddle text.

suggestions.title: No way to reset vibe streak manually — streak can only increment or break naturally
suggestions.description: The vibe streak counter (App.jsx line 1064-1066, stored in localStorage) only increments when vibe stays the same or resets to 1 when vibe changes. There is no affordance for a user to manually reset it (e.g., if they want to start fresh, or if the persisted value from another session is misleading). The streak eulogy toast ('RIP the streak') fires on automatic reset only. Consider: long-pressing or double-clicking the streak badge triggers a confirmation reset. Low effort, adds a satisfying interaction for the streak power users.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline legend pills use array index as React key
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 438: sparkline-legend__pill elements keyed by index i. The sparkline shows the last 3 vibes from history. When history shifts (new vibe added, old one dropped), React uses index-based reconciliation: it updates existing DOM nodes in place rather than re-ordering them. This causes the pill transition animations to play incorrectly — the wrong pill animates, or no animation fires when a new vibe is prepended. Fix: key by the vibe timestamp: key={h.ts} since each history entry has a unique ts (Date.now() at insert time, App.jsx line 1100).
